objective.p1: Durak means "Fool" in Russian. The last player left holding cards at the end of the game is the Durak — the loser. Everyone else wins by emptying their hand before the deck runs out.
objective.p2: There is no single winner — only one loser. First players to empty their hands leave the game safely; the final player holding cards takes the title.

deck.p1: The game uses a 42-card deck: four suits (♠ ♥ ♦ ♣) with ranks 7 8 9 10 J Q K A, plus 2 Jokers.
deck.p2: Cards 2–6 are removed. The remaining 10 ranks per suit (7 through Ace) form the standard hand, with Ace being the highest.
deck.jokerNote: Jokers beat everything — including trump cards.

trump.p1: Before the game, the bottom card of the deck is revealed face-up to determine the trump suit (Huzur). This card stays visible until the deck is exhausted, at which point it is drawn last.
trump.rule1: Any trump card beats any non-trump card, regardless of rank.
trump.rule2: To beat a trump card, you must play a higher trump card.
trump.rule3: Jokers beat everything, including the highest trump.
trump.rule4: If you have no valid defense card, you must pick up all table cards.

turn.title: Each Round
turn.step1: Attacker plays one or more cards of the same rank onto the table. Other players (co-attackers) may add cards matching any rank already on the table.
turn.step2: Defender must beat each attacking card: play a higher card of the same suit, or any trump card to beat a non-trump attack.
turn.step3: If the defender beats all cards, the round ends. All table cards are discarded from the game. The next player to the left becomes attacker.
turn.step4: If the defender cannot or chooses not to beat a card, they pick up all cards on the table. The attacker to their left becomes the next attacker.
turn.step5: After each round, all players draw back up to their starting hand size (attacker first, then defenders, then others), until the deck is empty.

stacking.p1: Co-attackers can throw in additional cards to the table as long as their rank matches a rank already on the table (either attacking cards or defending cards).
stacking.p2: The total number of cards the defender must beat cannot exceed their hand size. Once the limit is reached, no more cards can be added.
stacking.limitDesc: If the defender picks up, they must take ALL cards on the table — including any throw-ins. This is a common strategy to load up a struggling defender.

modes.classicDesc: 2–6 players. Everyone plays for themselves. Players who empty their hand leave the game safely. The last remaining player is the Durak.
modes.teamsDesc: 4 or 6 players split into two teams (alternating seats). Your team wins if none of your teammates are the final Durak.
modes.rankedDesc: Ranked games use Elo matchmaking — you're matched against players near your rating. Winning earns Elo; being the Durak costs Elo. Starting Elo is 1000.

jokers.p1: The deck contains 2 Jokers. A Joker beats any card — including the highest trump — and can be used to defend against any attack or as an attacking card of any rank.
jokers.p2: Jokers are extremely rare and powerful. Use them wisely: they are most valuable when you need to survive a dangerous round or guarantee a safe exit from the game.

tips.tip1: Save your trump cards — use non-trump to defend when possible.
tips.tip2: Attack with cards whose rank already appears on the table to enable teammates to throw in.
tips.tip3: When you have few cards, aim to make the current Durak pick up, not clear the table.
tips.tip4: Watch the Huzur suit — everyone's counting trump cards.
tips.tip5: If you can empty your hand in one shot, do it — don't hold back.
tips.tip6: Jokers are lifesavers: use them when you're about to be forced to pick up.
